_comment: Minimum read-only IAM policy for `cbx audit aws`. It authorizes two layers: (1) the explicit per-service AWS SDK calls the discovery code makes directly — STS preflight, ec2:DescribeRegions, the S3/IAM/KMS describers, the account-posture probes (ec2:GetEbsEncryptionByDefault, iam:GetAccountSummary, iam:GetAccountPasswordPolicy, the credential-report console-MFA probe (iam:GenerateCredentialReport + iam:GetCredentialReport — flags IAM users with a console password but no MFA, CIS 1.10 / FSBP IAM.5), glue:GetResourcePolicy for the per-region Glue Data Catalog resource policy), the observability-posture probes (guardduty:ListDetectors, guardduty:GetDetector, config:DescribeConfigurationRecorders, ec2:DescribeFlowLogs), the EBS snapshot lister/describer, the ApiGatewayV2 describer (apigatewayv2:GetStages — an apigateway:GET action, already granted below under CloudControlReadHandlers, since Cloud Control can't list the per-stage AccessLogSettings without the parent ApiId), the EKS cluster describer (iam:ListOpenIDConnectProviders — cross-references the cluster's OIDC issuer against registered IAM OIDC providers to detect whether IRSA is wired — plus a direct eks:ListPodIdentityAssociations call per cluster, the complementary probe that detects EKS Pod Identity, the OIDC-free way to grant pods scoped roles; this eks action needs no explicit grant here as it is already covered by the eks:List* wildcard in CloudControlReadHandlers below), the AWS Backup fallback listers + describers (the BackupReads statement: backup:ListBackupVaults / ListBackupPlans enumerate on a silent-empty Cloud Control list, backup:DescribeBackupVault / GetBackupVaultAccessPolicy / GetBackupPlan enrich vault-lock, access-policy and plan-rule state), the ECR repository fallback lister + lifecycle-policy probe (ecr:DescribeRepositories + ecr:GetLifecyclePolicy, granted under CloudControlReadHandlers), the per-bucket region resolution in the S3 fallback lister (s3:GetBucketLocation), and the Lambda reserved-concurrency probe (lambda:GetFunctionConcurrency); and (2) the Cloud Control discovery path (cloudformation:ListResources/GetResource), where Cloud Control invokes each CFN resource type's List/Read handler under the *caller's* identity, so the caller needs read access to every service in internal/audit/discover/aws/types_to_query.go (EC2, S3, IAM, RDS, Lambda, ECS, ECR, EKS, ELBv2, EFS, ApiGateway, DynamoDB, ElastiCache, Athena, SQS, SNS, Events, Cognito, WAFv2, ACM, Logs, CloudWatch, SecretsManager, KMS, CloudFront, Route53, CloudTrail, Backup). It is strictly read-only and deliberately avoids data-plane reads (e.g. secretsmanager:GetSecretValue, s3:GetObject). The AWS-managed AWSReadOnlyAccess policy is a valid superset; use this curated policy when a least-privilege grant is required. Plan §7.6.
